root_cause:
StdioTransport used Content-Length framing (RFC LSP wire format: 'Content-Length: N

{body}') but the official @modelcontextprotocol/sdk (used by @playwright/mcp, Claude Desktop, and all MCP SDK-based servers) uses newline-delimited JSON (NDJSON: one JSON object per line terminated by '
'). When AVA sent 'Content-Length: N

{body}', Playwright's ReadBuffer.readMessage() searched for '
', found it inside the '

' separator, parsed 'Content-Length: N' as JSON (which fails), silently discarded the message, and waited for more input. AVA's receive() then blocked indefinitely waiting for a response that would never come.
changes: Replaced send_framed/receive_framed with send_ndjson/receive_ndjson helpers in StdioTransport
StdioTransport.send() now calls send_ndjson (JSON + '\n', then flush)
StdioTransport.receive() now calls receive_ndjson (read_line, skip blanks, parse JSON)
Removed dead send_framed/receive_framed functions (FramedTransport has its own inline implementation)
Updated client_connects_to_mock_process_and_calls_tool integration test Python mock server from Content-Length framing to NDJSON
Added stdio_transport_with_playwright_mcp test that spawns real Playwright MCP server with 5s timeout
unaffected: FramedTransport (still uses Content-Length framing — used by AVAMCPServer which is AVA's own server, not an MCP SDK client)
